title: Turkey Chili
description: This hearty American-style turkey chili is the perfect comfort food for cold days. Packed with lean ground turkey, colorful vegetables, and warming spices, it delivers incredible flavor and nutrition in every bowl. The secret ingredient - masa harina - creates a rich, satisfying texture that will have you coming back for seconds.
ingredients: 2 cans (10 oz each) diced tomatoes with green chilies
1 (15 oz) can kidney beans (drained and rinsed)
1 (15 oz) can black beans (drained and rinsed)
instructions: Heat 1 Tablespoon olive oil over medium-high heat in a Dutch oven or large stockpot. Add 1 pound ground turkey and cook, breaking it into small pieces with a wooden spoon, until cooked through and no pink remains (about 5-7 minutes). Remove turkey from the pot and set aside on a plate. Drain any excess liquid from the pot.

Add the remaining 2 Tablespoons olive oil to the pot. Once the oil is hot and shimmering, add 1 diced onion, 3 diced celery ribs, 1/2 diced poblano pepper, and 1 shredded carrot. Cook for 4 minutes, stirring occasionally, then add 3 minced garlic cloves and cook 1 minute more until fragrant.

Meanwhile, whisk together 1/2 cup masa harina, 1 Tablespoon cumin, 1 Tablespoon garlic powder, 1 Tablespoon kosher salt, 2 teaspoons chili powder, 2 teaspoons ancho chili powder, 1 1/2 teaspoons paprika, 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon, and 1/2 teaspoon coriander in a small bowl.

Return the cooked turkey to the pot. Sprinkle the spice-masa mixture evenly over the turkey and vegetables. Stir well and cook for 1-2 minutes to toast the spices and allow the masa harina to begin absorbing moisture.

Add 2 cups chicken broth, 2 cans diced tomatoes with green chilies (with their juices), drained kidney beans, drained black beans, and creamed corn. Stir thoroughly to combine, making sure to scrape up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.

Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered for 20-30 minutes, stirring occasionally and scraping the bottom of the pot to prevent the masa harina from sticking and scorching. The chili will thicken as it simmers. If it becomes too thick, add a splash of broth or water.
Taste for seasoning and adjust salt or chili powder as needed.
Serve topped with sour cream, shredded cheddar cheese, diced green onions, and hot sauce, if desired.
notes: For gluten-free: This recipe is naturally gluten-free when using masa harina (make sure it's certified gluten-free if needed).
Salt level: 1 Tablespoon of kosher salt is appropriate if using Diamond Crystal brand. If using Morton's kosher salt or table salt, reduce to about 1 1/2-2 teaspoons, then adjust to taste.
Masa harina is the thickener here and gives the chili body and subtle corn flavor. Do NOT substitute cornmeal or corn starch as they behave differently.
Storage: Refrigerate for up to 4 days. Freezes well for up to 3 months. Thaw overnight in the fridge and reheat gently, adding a splash of broth if needed.
